title: quercus × macdonaldii
intro: Quercus × macdonaldii, also known as the Macdonald's oak hybrid, is a rare deciduous tree hybrid between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. The bark, leaves, and acorns of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × macdonaldii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are typically consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
